Although the candidates and their controlled ba11ot measure

|state law requires proponents to turn their signatures into
any given County at the same tirne. Elections Code S 3520(a).

sections.question:
1) Will the Committee have made or received an in-kind contribution from the other candidate controlled ballot measure committees as a result of the consultation and coordination between the committees?
2) If the answer to question #1 is yes are such in-kind contributions permissible under the Political Reform Act?
3) Assuming the answer to question #2 is yes, how should the in-kind contributions be reported?
4) Assuming the answer to question #2 is no, would the answer be different if the candidates and their controlled ballot measure committees acted independently of each other except to the extent they coordinated the submission of signatures to the county clerks?
sections.question_synthetic: The requester seeks FPPC advice regarding whether coordination and consultation among multiple candidate-controlled ballot measure committees supporting the same initiative will result in in-kind contributions, whether such contributions are permissible, and how they should be reported under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. Document contains four specific legal questions about in-kind contributions and coordination among candidate-controlled committees. No verbatim conclusion section exists as this is the initial request, not the FPPC's response letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1) Will the Committee have made or received an in-kind contribution from the other candidate controlled ballot measure committees as a result of the consultation and coordination between the committees?
2) If the answer to question #1 is yes are such in-kind contributions permissible under the Political Reform Act?
3) Assuming the answer to question #2 is yes, how should the in-kind contributions be reported?
4) Assuming the answer to question #2 is no, would the answer be different if the candidates and their controlled ballot measure committees acted independently of each other except to the extent they coordinated the submission of signatures to the county clerks?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from counsel representing Assemblymember Tom Hayden and the California Environmental Protection Act of 1990 Hayden Committee, seeking FPPC advice on the campaign reporting obligations and in-kind contribution implications when multiple candidate-controlled ballot measure committees coordinate efforts to support the same initiative measure.
